prompt: Kuukikan (空気感) style hyper-realistic film photograph. IDENTICAL composition to IP-01 — same California suburban kitchen, same early summer morning, same half-open stainless-steel refrigerator with perfectly organized interior (yogurt, salmon, vegetables all stocked). But this time, subtle differences signal one year of lived-in trust: on the dining table, a cup of green tea with visible steam rising gently into the morning light. Through the window, lush California early-summer greenery is visible — fuller and more vibrant than before. The kitchen feels slightly more 'inhabited' — a small potted herb on the windowsill, a child's drawing held by a magnet on the fridge door. No people present. The same quiet stillness, but warmer. Light: same soft diffused morning window light as IP-01, but shifted subtly warmer — a touch more golden, as if the kitchen has 'settled in.' Color grading: same film-like desaturation as IP-01 but with a barely perceptible warm yellow shift. The tea steam catches light beautifully. Same spectral refraction on glass shelves (hidden rainbow hint). Composition: 16:9. EXACTLY the same framing as IP-01 — refrigerator at left third, window and kitchen as generous negative space. The tea cup on the table is the only new element. Camera: same 35mm lens, f/4.0, ISO 200. Same fine film grain. The mood shifts from IP-01's 'serene completion' to 'gentle domesticity' — the same scene, but now with life woven into it. The difference between a model home and a lived-in home. A year of trust made visible through warmth.
negative_prompt: dramatic lighting, lens flare, HDR, oversaturated, CGI, plastic look, studio lighting, people, human figures, dark room, text overlays, watermarks, logos, AI smoothing, digital painting, anime, cartoon, illustration, cold sterile look
